nodes.title:
Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-
Intensive NLP Tasks

metadata.evidence_sentence: To mitigate this challenge, the Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) paradigm [7] has been introduced, integrating arXiv:2311.16543v3 [cs.AR] 20 May 2024 retrieval mechanisms to improve the precision of generated content by incorporating information from external knowledge sources.
evidence_sentences: To mitigate this challenge, the Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) paradigm [7] has been introduced, integrating arXiv:2311.16543v3 [cs.AR] 20 May 2024 retrieval mechanisms to improve the precision of generated content by incorporating information from external knowledge sources.

2.3 Retrieval-Augmented Generation Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) [7] represents a significant advancement in addressing the limitations of Large Language Mod- els when handling knowledge-intensive tasks.
metadata.reason: Section 'introduction' and keyword 'introduced' indicate a foundational history citation.

metadata.evidence_sentence: GitHub Copilot [4], building upon such groundwork, played a crucial role in pioneering LLM-based code completion engines, contributing significantly to the domains of auto-completion and conversational code gener- ation.

nodes.title:
ReACC: A Retrieval-Augmented Code Completion Frame-
work

metadata.evidence_sentence: In the context of code generation, several works such as ReACC [10] and RepoCoder [21] have successfully harnessed the capabilities of RAG to enhance the code generation proficiency of LLMs, showcasing its transformative potential.
evidence_sentences: In the context of code generation, several works such as ReACC [10] and RepoCoder [21] have successfully harnessed the capabilities of RAG to enhance the code generation proficiency of LLMs, showcasing its transformative potential.

nodes.title:
ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language
Models

metadata.evidence_sentence: The ReAct framework [20] integrates reasoning and action synthesis in language models, demonstrating LLM’s capability to engage in reasoning processes and refine decision-making through interactive feedback.
evidence_sentences: The ReAct framework [20] integrates reasoning and action synthesis in language models, demonstrating LLM’s capability to engage in reasoning processes and refine decision-making through interactive feedback.

3.2 Reasoning and Action Planning through ReAct Iterative Prompting We enable Large Language Models to function as autonomous agents for reasoning and action planning through theReActprompt- ing mechanism [20].
